assumptions_to_verify: fixtures invoke the real reviewer_panel_eval_runner at runtime to derive weights (supported by reading _calibration() :22-102 and :327 bodies)

whether 0.65 is a dependence weight (it is the allowed severity-policy multiplier, distinct from data-derived dependence weight)
whether tests are vacuous (refuted: :327 contrasts independent vs correlated derived weights; :128 asserts None+conservative)
missing_evidence: pytest run output confirming tests pass and fixtures derive weights at runtime
direct read of the 4 workflow test bodies (:3913/:3955/:3981/:4005) this round (relied on byte-identical prior verification)
critical_review.strongest_objection: The decisive weights (0.05) and severity multiplier (0.65) could be hardcoded constants in fixtures, violating the measured-not-constants acceptance.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: If pytest showed any of the 9 tests failing/erroring, or if a fixture were found to inject reviewer_weights as a literal rather than via reviewer_panel_eval_runner output, I would move to revise.
